title: Reverse a C-style String
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read a word into a C-style string (a null-terminated `char` array), report its length, then build a new C-style string holding the characters in reverse order. You are responsible for placing the null terminator correctly on the reversed string.

## Input
A single line containing one word (no spaces, at most 40 characters).

## Output
- Line 1: the length (as `std::strlen` would report -- the null terminator is not counted).
- Line 2: the reversed string.

Do not use `std::string`, `std::reverse`, or any `<algorithm>` function.

## Example
Input `hello` produces:
```
5
olleh
```

title: Compare and Concatenate C-style Strings
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read two words into C-style strings, determine their lexicographic order with `std::strcmp`, then concatenate them (first followed by second, no separator) and report the result's length and the joined string. Build the concatenation yourself with the `<cstring>` copy/append functions.

## Input
- Line 1: a word (no spaces, at most 30 characters).
- Line 2: a word (no spaces, at most 30 characters).

## Output
- Line 1: `before`, `after`, or `equal` -- the order of the first string relative to the second.
- Line 2: the length of the concatenated string.
- Line 3: the concatenated string.

Do not use `std::string` or any `<algorithm>` function.

## Example
Inputs `apple` and `banana` produce:
```
before
11
applebanana
```

test_cases.label: Hidden -- shared prefix orders by length
